beats.text: A forest scene: trees, fungi, deer, mice, and one MIT student walking through it. This whole system is the ecosystem.
Zoom in on the student. She is the organism — one living thing inside that ecosystem.
Pull into her eye. The eye is an organ. Inside it, the retina — a tissue made of layers of cells packed together.
Zoom into one single cell in the retina: a photoreceptor. This is the cell level.
Inside that photoreceptor, small bean-shaped structures appear — mitochondria. These are organelles.

A single ATP molecule assembles inside a mitochondrion. That's the molecule level — the smallest rung on the ladder. Ecosystem, organism, organ, tissue, cell, organelle, molecule. Every level, nested inside the last.
